So we got them persuaded to make the appointment. We have them persuaded of nothing else. And now we are going to show up, and on the strength of our salesmanship, we are going to make our case and make our sale. That shift has been to put something in place in between, and actually intentionally, deliberately slow down the sales process. So I talked the other day to, and I won't identify her, because some of you would know her, but I talked the other day to a woman who for the first time is considering some cosmetic procedures. And so, and this is instructive for you, because this is also somebody who should have a more sophisticated approach to choosing whoever is going to cut and paste them. So she has picked the person by their magazine ad in a magazine that is all paid content. And so there's a big article, and there's a two-page beautiful ad and all that. So she's picked this doctor. All sides to be a woman doctor, which is also, I think, instructive. As an aside, Citi Barrow did this big research project that never got published for a publishing company on cosmetic surgeons and their patients. And she tracked, she's like 100 patients from their first visit all the way through completion, met with them over and over and over again, interviewed them. And one of the things, the biggest thing that many of them expressed distress about, was not anything about the quality of the procedures, by the way. It was having to sit and talk to women significantly younger than they. So I don't want to talk to a 23-year-old flawless girl when I am coming and going and getting the after surgery stuff I need done and whatever. And so all you got to do is change out the age of the people who are interacting with them and you instantly make the practice better. So anyway, so she has picked them from this magazine ad, and she is now called to make the appointment. And so I said, so how are they on the phone? Really good. Here's what they did on the phone. What questions did you ask them? And what were their answers, right? And the answers are really, really good. So the answers were really, really good. And now this is, let's see, this is like a week ago. So this is, what's the date today? 31st of July. This is 31st of July. Okay. This is like a week ago. I said, so when's your appointment? Because this person is ready now, you understand. They're ready. So they're going to go in for the appointment and they're going to computer image and show what you're going to look like and hopefully, unless you happen to go to like say the doc who did Bert Reynolds. But I said, so when's your appointment? Well, the earliest I can get in is August 20, whatever. Right? Well, that's bullshit. All right? I mean, this ain't Beverly Hills. Okay? This person's in Pittsburgh. All right? I mean, please. Okay? And there's like 30 of them in the magazine in Pittsburgh alone. God knows how many of them there are in Pittsburgh, but there's 30 of them in this magazine that she has picked from and she's in Pittsburgh. You got to tell me this woman doesn't have an appointment time until, for a month? Nuts. This woman knows what she's doing. And that question is, are they sending you anything before the appointment? You bet. Right? Well, the appointment's being deliberately delayed in order to send the stuff. Now, that takes courage because the normal sales instinct is, they raise their hand a little, pull it up the rest of the way up, throw them on the ground and take their wallet. Right? That's the normal, that's like the norm before they change their mind. You know? Or before somebody else gets to them or before, right? No, this has been deliberately delayed. Okay? And now my wager is, based on that window, there's going to be more than one thing sent during that time period. I said, so what did they tell you is coming? Well, there's two DVDs.
